reviews.review_description:
Don’t get me wrong it’s a good one… but $20 is in my opinion a little steep for what little there is to it. Think of a simple little toy a mom would give her kid to fiddle with while she checks out at the grocery store… this game is kinda like that toy.

There are the spells and a room of stuff to kinda toss around… and that’s it. 

Im looking forward to where it goes tho… because what is there is great. I love how the magic isn’t some overly showy cinematic mumbo jumbo- the time reset spell is a simple snap and instantaneous reset. Makes the magic feel realistic.

This is a brilliant example of what can be achieved on the Quest platform when a developer takes a more bespoke approach. This wonderful little physics playground is incredibly performant (120Hz on Quest 3), has super clean visuals and a variety of fun little mechanics. I look forward to seeing future experiences get built on this base.

It's fun to experiment with all the options. But you are going to add hand tracking, right? The hand gestures and everything are really asking for it, and it would be a ton of fun doing all that "magic" with hand gestures. This game would become a must have for everyone that wants to show VR to new players. Keep up the good work!
Fire goes boom boom! Also the physics are extremely impressive, and graphics as well! Very impressed!
Lovely game. I just whish there was music as as shown in the video.  and mixed reality would be brilliant
